title: crataegus crus-galli subsp. berberifolia
intro: Crataegus crus-galli subsp. berberifolia, also known as the Berberis-leaved hawthorn, is a subspecies of the hawthorn plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Asia, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Berberis-leaved hawthorn has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as cardiovascular issues, digestive problems, and high blood pressure, thanks to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve blood lipid profiles, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Crataegus crus-galli subsp. berberifolia typically involve infusions, decoctions, or extracts of the leaves, flowers, and fruits, which can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or capsules.
